California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Howard L. McCalla 
2650 Calle De Compadres 
Alpine, CA 
92001 
Dear Mr. McCalla: 
November 15, 1989 
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-89-565 
You have requested advice under the conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").l 
The Commission's Legal Division provides written advice to 
persons with questions about their duties under the Act. 
We do 
not provide advice to third parties about another person's duties 
or obligations unless the third party has been authorized to 
request advice by the person whose duties or obligations are in 
question. 
(Section 83114, Regulation 18329; copy enclosed.) 
Therefore, we cannot provide advice on the question you have 
raised. 
However, if you believe a violation of the Act has 
occurred, you may contact our Enforcement Division at 
(916) 322-6441. 
A copy of a complaint for is enclosed for your 
convenience. 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E.

f his appointment ,.2,,1.;:

HO,\VARD L. ~lCCALLA 
2650 CALLE DE CO~fPADIU:""; 
ALPr:-:E. CALIf'. 9:2001 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Attention: 
John Larsen, Chairman 
428 "J" Street -
Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
SUBJECT: 
Request for ruling on application of the term 
"assuming office" [Gov. Code Section 87302(b) J. 
Dear Mr. Larsen, 
On July 18, 1989 the San Diego County Board of Supervisors 
filled an existing vacancy on the Alpine Planning Group by 
appointing an Alpine resident named Uif Brynjestad. 
[See 
ttached Board minute order 966.] 
Financial disclosure state-
ments are required under Section 87302(b) "within 30 days of 
assuming office .•. ". 
The purpose of this letter is to request 
an PPPC ruling on when this appointee is deemed to have 
"assumed office", 
Was it the date of his appointment or was it 
the date he attended his first meeting as a member. 
The appointee attended his first meeting on July 27, 1989, 
the regular meeting for that month. 
The Alpine Planning Group 
has held many special meetings and if one had been scheduled 
between July 18 and July 27 the appointee would have been on 
the Group Membership Roll as a member. 
Planning Group Members are not "sworn in" nor are they 
required to sign any oath of office. 
The·"sole authority for 
the existence and functioning of the Alpine Planning Group [one 
of many in San Diego County] is in Board of Supervisors Policy 
I-I, a copy of which is attached. 
The County's planning groups 
are part-time citizen advisory bodies elected by local regis-
tered voters and their members serve four year terms [Policy 
I-I, pp. 2,3, & 4]. 
Planning Group Members have been made 
subject to the Political Reform Act financial disclosure re-
quirements [Policy I-I, pp. 4 & 5J. 
Relevant references to th 
filling of Planning Group 
vacanC1es are on pages 13 and 14 of Policy I-I. 
Please note 
that Article II, Section III [page 13] states: 
"Appointees to vacanC1es 
hall serve out the full 
unexpired term of the vac nt seat," [emphasis added]. 


A ruling on when this appointee "assumed office" would be 
appreciated. 
Attachments: 
Very truly yours, 
Board Minute Order #66 -
July 18, 1989 
Board of Supervisors Policy I-I 
.' 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Howard L. McCalla 
250 Calle De Compadres 
Alpine, CA 
92001 
Dear Mr. McCalla: 
October 2, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-565 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on September 29, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact Jonathan Rothman an attorney in the Legal 
Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt ofa proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh 
Very truly yours, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660
